project.name: English Vault: Tokyo Debug Chronicles
project.websiteGoal: Comic episode -> English Focus -> Pattern card -> Practice prompt -> Review mode
project.mainPromise: Learn English patterns through stories and reuse them across travel, software engineering, Japan life, daily life, and work.

packs.title: Decision / Hesitation
packs.purpose: Use when the learner is unsure, choosing between options, or asking for advice.
patterns: I don’t know what to + verb.
I don’t know whether to A or B.
I don’t know if I should A or B.
I can’t decide what to + verb.
I can’t decide between A and B.
I have no idea what / which / how to...
I’m torn between A and B.
I’m debating whether to A or B.
packs.example: I’m debating whether to deploy today or wait for QA.

packs.title: Preference / Opinion
packs.purpose: Use when the learner wants to say what they prefer, what makes sense, or what option they choose.
patterns: I like A more than B.
A makes more sense to me.
I think A is better because...
I think I’ll go with A.
packs.masterCombo: I’m torn between A and B, but I’m leaning toward A because A makes more sense to me. I think I’ll go with A.

packs.title: Bug Report + Agreement / Disagreement + Through Verbs
packs.purpose: Use for software engineering communication: reporting bugs, disagreeing politely, explaining risk, and proposing safe next steps.
packs.masterCombo: I see your point, but the issue is that the API is returning incorrect data for some users. It seems like this might be caused by the new caching logic. The bug affects the payment flow, so I think we should wait for QA before deploying.

packs.title: Universal Communication Pack V2
packs.purpose: Use across travel, software engineering, Japan life, daily life, work meetings, learning, customer support, Japanese culture, and anime/manga exploration.

groups.title: Asking for Help / Clarification
patterns: Could you help me with this?
Can you show me how to + verb?
Could you explain this part again?
Just to clarify, do you mean A or B?
Let me make sure I got this right.
Could you speak a little more slowly?

groups.title: Scheduling / Appointment
patterns: What time works best for you?
Can we move it to tomorrow?
Can we push it back by 30 minutes?

groups.title: Daily Life Survival
patterns: I need to fill out this form.

packs.title: Email & Async English
packs.purpose: Use when writing email, Slack, async standups, follow-ups, and keeping stakeholders in the loop.
patterns: I wanted to check in on...
Could you loop me in on...?
I'll get back to you by...
packs.masterCombo: Just following up on the PR I sent yesterday — I wanted to check in on the review timeline. I'm blocked on staging access, so I'll follow up with DevOps and get back to you by EOD. Could you loop me in if anything changes?

packs.title: Interview & Career English
packs.purpose: Use in job interviews, career conversations, and professional self-introduction.
patterns: Tell me about a time when...
What I learned from that was...
I'm looking for a role where...
Why I'm interested in this role is...
What attracted me to this company is...
Do you have any questions for us?
packs.masterCombo: Tell me about a time when our payment API went down before a demo. One challenge I faced was fixing it under time pressure. What I learned from that was to always run through the main flow early. I'm looking for a role where I can grow as a bridge between engineering and product.

packs.title: Negotiation & Boundaries
packs.purpose: Use when negotiating deadlines, salary, scope, and setting professional boundaries.
patterns: I'm concerned that the timeline is too tight.
Could we push the deadline back by...?
I'd be more comfortable if we...
I'm happy to help, but we'll need to...
Let me push back on that.
packs.masterCombo: I'm concerned that the timeline is too tight for a full refactor. Would it be possible to push the deadline back by one week? That's outside the original scope, so I'd need at least two more days for QA. I'm happy to help, but we'll need to narrow down the requirements first.

packs.title: Giving Feedback & 1-on-1
packs.purpose: Use in 1-on-1 meetings, performance reviews, and giving constructive feedback.
patterns: One thing that went well was...
One area to improve would be...
What would help you most right now?
Let's set a goal for next week.
packs.masterCombo: One thing that went well was how you handled the incident calmly. One area to improve would be writing clearer PR descriptions. I'd suggest trying a short summary at the top of each PR. How can I support you with that this week?

packs.title: Presentation & Pitch English
packs.purpose: Use when presenting demos, pitching ideas, or explaining to PM and stakeholders.
patterns: The main problem we're solving is...
Here's what changed since last time.
As you can see on this slide...
packs.masterCombo: I'll keep this brief. Let me walk you through the new checkout flow. The main problem we're solving is failed payments during peak traffic. The key takeaway is that caching cut response time by 40%. Any questions on this part?

packs.title: Incident & Postmortem English
packs.purpose: Use in war rooms, incident updates, and blameless postmortem meetings.
patterns: What we know so far is...
To prevent this from happening again...
Let's focus on the facts, not blame.
packs.masterCombo: What we know so far is that checkout failed for about 12% of users. Root cause appears to be a cache invalidation bug. We mitigated the issue by rolling back the release. Action items going forward: add an integration test and update the runbook.

packs.title: Get Phrasal Verbs — Startup & Tech
packs.purpose: Use in Silicon Valley / startup work: Slack, standups, ship day, war rooms, career moments.
patterns: Can you loop me in on...?
I need to get up to speed on...
Can you help me get unblocked?
I got pulled into the war room.
I'll get back to you by...
packs.masterCombo: Can you loop me in on the launch channel? I'm getting up to speed but I'm blocked on the payments API. Once I get unblocked I'll get through review — we need buy-in from legal before we get shipped. I'll get back to you after standup.

packs.title: Get Phrasal Verbs — Travel & Movement
packs.purpose: Use when traveling: trains, hotels, directions, weather delays, exploring new places.
patterns: I need to get checked in.
Let me get my bearings first.
Where can I get a ticket?
Which platform do we get on?
Don't forget to get off at...
We got snowed in last night.
Sorry — I got held up by the train.
I can get by with phrasebook English.
packs.masterCombo: How do I get to the hotel? I'll get checked in first and get my bearings in the old town. We got a ticket for the Glacier Express — get on at platform 7 and get off at Filisur. We got snowed in at Zermatt but we can get by until the trains run again.

packs.title: Get Phrasal Verbs — Everyday English
packs.purpose: Use when learning English daily: meetups, homework, building confidence.
patterns: I got through my first presentation.
I'll get back to studying after dinner.
My English is good enough to get by.
packs.masterCombo: I'm still getting used to speaking English at meetups. Let me get ready — I need to get through this lesson first. Oh, I get it now! I'll get back to homework after work. My English isn't perfect, but I can get by.

sampleEpisode.title: Quick Patch or Full Refactor?
sampleEpisode.arc: Career Growth Arc — Nam Speaks Up at Work
patterns: I’m torn between A and B.
A makes more sense to me.
englishFocus: I’m torn between A and B.
A makes more sense to me.
sampleEpisode.hook: Next: Nam has to demo the feature under pressure.

reviewModeTemplate: Bật English Vault Review Mode. Topic: [selected topic]. Use the patterns from this episode. Ask me one Vietnamese situation at a time. I will answer in English. Correct grammar, word choice, tense, prepositions, articles, and natural phrasing immediately. Explain mistakes in simple Vietnamese. Give a natural/native version. Make me reuse the corrected pattern in a new context. After 10 questions, summarize my repeated mistakes and mini homework.
